$schema: Frontier Firm Scorecard v4 — Two Lanes × Three Pillars
description: 2 lanes (M365 Copilot, Agents) × 3 pillars (Skill, Consistency, Reach) = 13 metrics. Pattern = weaker lane.

copilot.label: M365 Copilot
copilot.description: Individual AI productivity — is Copilot becoming part of how people work?

agents.label: Agents
agents.description: Team-level AI — are agents embedded in real workflows?

copilot_app_breadth.name: App Surface Breadth
copilot_app_breadth.description: Avg distinct M365 app surfaces used per user

copilot_multiturn.name: Deep Interactions
copilot_multiturn.description: % of sessions (ThreadIds) with 5+ prompt messages — measures depth of multi-turn engagement

copilot_habitual.name: Habitual User Rate
copilot_habitual.description: Adaptive: 3+ months = avg 6+ days AND present 2/3 months; 2 months = avg 6+ days AND present in both; 1 month = 16+ active days

copilot_retention.name: Licensed User Return Rate
copilot_retention.description: % of licensed M365 Copilot users (all activity types incl. chat, agents, apps) who return the following month

copilot_activation.name: License Activation
copilot_activation.description: % of assigned Copilot licenses that are actively used

copilot_coverage.name: License Coverage
copilot_coverage.description: % of total active users who hold a license

agent_return.name: Agent User Return Rate
agent_return.description: % of agent users who return the following month — measures whether the agent-using population as a whole is coming back, not per-agent stickiness. Derived from agent_retention (never extracted separately).

agent_habitual.name: Agent Habitual Rate
agent_habitual.description: % of agent users with 6+ active days per month

agent_retention.name: Agent User Return Rate
agent_retention.description: % of agent users specifically who return the following month — typically higher than licensed return rate as agent users are power users

agent_adoption.name: Agent Adoption
agent_adoption.description: % of active users engaging with agents

agent_org_penetration.name: Org Penetration
agent_org_penetration.description: % of active orgs where BOTH licensed Copilot users AND agent users are present

pattern_rules.description: Pattern determined by the weaker lane. Each lane tier = weakest pillar. Each pillar tier = weakest metric.

company_pattern_gates.description: Company-level P1/P2/P3 pattern profile classification. Patterns are hierarchical — P3 requires P2 ≥ Nascent, P2 requires P1 ≥ Nascent. To change thresholds edit this file — no code change needed.

p1.rule: Nascent: activation >= 40%. Primary: also habitual licensed users >= 30%.

p2.rule: Nascent: agent adoption >= 5%. Primary: also agent user return >= 40%. Requires P1 >= Nascent.

p3.rule: Nascent: agent habitual rate >= 15%. Primary: also >= 50% of orgs at 10% agent adoption. Requires P2 >= Nascent.

org_pattern_gates.description: Unified gate logic applied identically to every org row and the OVERALL company row. Both thresholds must be met to qualify for a level. No averaging — a miss on either metric holds the classification at the lower level.

habit.population: All active users — M365 Copilot licensed users AND unlicensed Copilot Chat users
habit.activity: Copilot Chat sessions only (not agent sessions)
habit.definition: % of all active users averaging 6+ Copilot Chat active days/month for 2+ consecutive months
habit.note: Denominator is total_active_users (licensed + unlicensed). Per-org habitual_pct is estimated from licensed habitual rate + unlicensed rate derived from company average.

agent.population: All active users — M365 Copilot licensed users AND unlicensed Copilot Chat users
agent.definition: % of all active users who engaged with at least one agent in the period
agent.note: Denominator is total_active_users (licensed + unlicensed). This is NOT % of licensed users — e.g. ZYX: 180 agent users / 4,201 total = 4.3%, whereas 180 / 1,616 licensed = 11.1%.

p2_expansion.rule: habit >= 15% AND agent >= 5%  (both as % of all active users)

p3_frontier.rule: habit >= 30% AND agent >= 15% (both as % of all active users)

materiality_thresholds.description: Minimum size an agent, org, or cohort must reach before it can be cited as a best-practice example, model, or recommendation target. Applies to both generated HTML (spotlight, depth chart) and AI narrative (insights). Edit these values to change what counts as material.

agents.share_denominator: agent_users (total users who engaged with any agent in the period)
agents.rule: agent.users >= min_users_absolute AND agent.users / agent_users * 100 >= min_users_share_pct
agents.note: Both conditions must be met. A single-user agent with 400 sessions/user is an outlier — frame as proof-of-concept depth, not a model to replicate.

orgs.note: Prevents naming a 50-person org as a role model in a 5,000-person company purely on rate metrics.
